reasoning: John Morgan has teased a gubernatorial run and even floated creating a new political party. While Florida law requires him to register for a new party 365 days before the June 2026 qualifying deadline (meaning by June 12, 2025), two factors strongly weigh against an announcement of candidacy before July 1, 2025. First, Morgan has a long track record of floating political runs without following through. Second, he explicitly stated in mid-May 2025 that he would prefer a 'three-month sprint' over an '18-month marathon', indicating he wants to delay any official campaign as long as possible. Even if he establishes a new party in June 2025, he does not need to formally announce his own candidacy at that time. With less than a month remaining before the July 1 deadline and no recent steps taken, the probability of him formally declaring is low.
